revision.text:
{{Short description|Legendary sumo wrestler}} [[File:野見宿禰.jpg|thumb|200px|Nomi no Sukune by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]]]]
{{nihongo|'''Nomi no Sukune'''|野見 宿禰}} was a Japanese legendary figure who is regarded as the founder of [[sumo]] wrestling.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book |url=https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/33014222 |title=Asashi Nihon rekishi jinbutsu jiten |date=1994 |publisher=Asahi Shinbunsha |isbn=4-02-340052-1 |at=藤原光能 |oclc=33014222}}</ref> He was the founder of the Haji no [[Muraji]] clan.<ref name=":3">{{Cite book |title=Nihon dai hyakka zensho |publisher=Shōgakkan |year=1989 |isbn=4-09-526001-7 |at=野見宿禰 |oclc=14970117}}</ref>
== Life == [[File:Nomi no Sukune Wrestling with Taima no Kehaya LACMA M.84.31.87.jpg|thumb|Nomi no Sukune wrestling with Taima no Kehaya (by [[Yoshitoshi]])]] Sukune is said to have lived during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] (29 BC – 70).<ref name="katano">{{cite web |title=The history of Kitano Jinja |url=http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |url-status=dead |publisher=Kitano Jinja |accessdate=2008-02-19 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080219104915/http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |archivedate=February 19, 2008}}</ref> Allegedly, in 23 B.C., the Emperor instructed Nomi no Sukune to deal with {{nihongo|Taima no Kehaya|当麻蹴速}} after he boasted that he was the strongest man "under the heavens." Nomi no Sukune engaged Taima no Kuyahaya in hand-to-hand combat and broke his ribs with one kick and his back with another, killing Taima no Kehaya.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Guttmann|first1=Allen|last2=Thompson|first2=Lee|title=Japanese Sports: A History|url=https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt|url-access=limited|date=2001|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|location=Honolulu, HI|isbn=9780824824143|page=[https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt/page/n24 14]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.essortment.com/wrestlingsumoh_rvqp.htm|title=Sumo wrestling history, the Ancient Japanese sport|publisher=Essortment|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> It was not modern sumo, but he is regarded as the creator of sumo.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm|title=History of Sumo Wrestling|publisher=Houston Chinatown|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref><!-- He is also regarded as the human creator of [[jujutsu]].<ref name="aikido">{{cite web|url=http://www.aikido-shobukan.org/books/View.aspx?id=330|title=Canon of Judo "Principle and Technique"|author=Mifune, Kiyuzo|work=Aikido Shobukan Dojo|date=1959|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> -->He was an ancestor of [[Sugawara no Michizane]],<ref name="katano" /> great-grandfather to Haji no Mino and was the founder of the Haji clan after his creation of ''[[haniwa]].''<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nomi no Sukune • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/|access-date=2021-09-01|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>
== Legacy == A mural of him, removed from the old [[National Olympic Stadium (Tokyo)|National Olympic Stadium]] in 2014, will be put on display at the new stadium in 2019.<ref>{{cite news|title=東京五輪向け壁画取り外し…建て替え国立競技場|url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/olympic/2020/20141105-OYT1T50114.html?from=ycont_top_txt|accessdate=8 November 2014|work=Yomiuri Shinbun|date=5 November 2014}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
[[Category:People of Yayoi-period Japan]] [[Category:Japanese sumo wrestlers]] [[Category:Japanese jujutsuka]] [[Category:Deified Japanese people]]
== External links == * [https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/ Nomi no Sukune] - ''History of Japan Website.''
{{Shinto-stub}} {{sumo-bio-stub}} {{japan-hist-stub}} {{japan-sport-bio-stub}} [[Category:Deified Japanese people in the Kiki]]

{{Short description|Legendary sumo wrestler}} [[File:野見宿禰.jpg|thumb|200px|Nomi no Sukune by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]]]]
{{nihongo|'''Nomi no Sukune'''|野見 宿禰}} was a Japanese legendary figure who is regarded as the founder of [[sumo]] wrestling.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book |url=https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/33014222 |title=Asashi Nihon rekishi jinbutsu jiten |date=1994 |publisher=Asahi Shinbunsha |isbn=4-02-340052-1 |at=藤原光能 |oclc=33014222}}</ref> He was the founder of the Haji no [[Muraji]] clan.<ref name=":3">{{Cite book |title=Nihon dai hyakka zensho |publisher=Shōgakkan |year=1989 |isbn=4-09-526001-7 |at=野見宿禰 |oclc=14970117}}</ref>
== Life == [[File:Nomi no Sukune Wrestling with Taima no Kehaya LACMA M.84.31.87.jpg|thumb|Nomi no Sukune wrestling with Taima no Kehaya (by [[Yoshitoshi]])]] Sukune is said to have lived during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] (29 BC – 70).<ref name="katano">{{cite web |title=The history of Kitano Jinja |url=http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |url-status=dead |publisher=Kitano Jinja |accessdate=2008-02-19 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080219104915/http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |archivedate=February 19, 2008}}</ref> Allegedly, in 23 B.C., the Emperor instructed Nomi no Sukune to deal with {{nihongo|Taima no Kehaya|当麻蹴速}} after he boasted that he was the strongest man "under the heavens." Nomi no Sukune engaged Taima no Kuyahaya in hand-to-hand combat and broke his ribs with one kick and his back with another, killing Taima no Kehaya.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Guttmann|first1=Allen|last2=Thompson|first2=Lee|title=Japanese Sports: A History|url=https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt|url-access=limited|date=2001|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|location=Honolulu, HI|isbn=9780824824143|page=[https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt/page/n24 14]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.essortment.com/wrestlingsumoh_rvqp.htm|title=Sumo wrestling history, the Ancient Japanese sport|publisher=Essortment|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> It was not modern sumo, but he is regarded as the creator of sumo.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm|title=History of Sumo Wrestling|publisher=Houston Chinatown|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref><!-- He is also regarded as the human creator of [[jujutsu]].<ref name="aikido">{{cite web|url=http://www.aikido-shobukan.org/books/View.aspx?id=330|title=Canon of Judo "Principle and Technique"|author=Mifune, Kiyuzo|work=Aikido Shobukan Dojo|date=1959|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> -->
He was an ancestor of [[Sugawara no Michizane]],<ref name="katano" /> great-grandfather to Haji no Mino and was the founder of the Haji clan after his creation of ''[[haniwa]].''<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nomi no Sukune • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/|access-date=2021-09-01|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>
== Legacy == A mural of him, removed from the old [[National Olympic Stadium (Tokyo)|National Olympic Stadium]] in 2014, will be put on display at the new stadium in 2019.<ref>{{cite news|title=東京五輪向け壁画取り外し…建て替え国立競技場|url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/olympic/2020/20141105-OYT1T50114.html?from=ycont_top_txt|accessdate=8 November 2014|work=Yomiuri Shinbun|date=5 November 2014}}</ref>

He was an ancestor of [[Sugawara no Michizane]],<ref name="katano" /> great-grandfather to Haji no Mino and was the founder of the Haji clan after his creation of ''[[haniwa]].''<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nomi no Sukune • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/|access-date=2021-09-01|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>

Nomi no Sukune engaged Taima no Kuyahaya in hand-to-hand combat and broke his ribs with one kick and his back with another, killing Taima no Kehaya.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Guttmann|first1=Allen|last2=Thompson|first2=Lee|title=Japanese Sports: A History|url=https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt|url-access=limited|date=2001|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|location=Honolulu, HI|isbn=9780824824143|page=[https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt/page/n24 14]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.essortment.com/wrestlingsumoh_rvqp.htm|title=Sumo wrestling history, the Ancient Japanese sport|publisher=Essortment|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> It was not modern sumo, but he is regarded as the creator of sumo.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm|title=History of Sumo Wrestling|publisher=Houston Chinatown|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref><!-- He is also regarded as the human creator of [[jujutsu]].<ref name="aikido">{{cite web|url=http://www.aikido-shobukan.org/books/View.aspx?id=330|title=Canon of Judo "Principle and Technique"|author=Mifune, Kiyuzo|work=Aikido Shobukan Dojo|date=1959|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> -->
He was an ancestor of [[Sugawara no Michizane]],<ref name="katano" /> great-grandfather to Haji no Mino and was the founder of the Haji clan after his creation of ''[[haniwa]].''<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nomi no Sukune • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/|access-date=2021-09-01|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>

He was an ancestor of [[Sugawara no Michizane]],<ref name="katano" /> great-grandfather to Haji no Mino and was the founder of the Haji clan after his creation of ''[[haniwa]].''<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nomi no Sukune • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/|access-date=2021-09-01|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>
== Legacy == A mural of him, removed from the old [[National Olympic Stadium (Tokyo)|National Olympic Stadium]] in 2014, was put on display at the new stadium in 2019.<ref>{{cite news|title=東京五輪向け壁画取り外し…建て替え国立競技場|url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/olympic/2020/20141105-OYT1T50114.html?from=ycont_top_txt|accessdate=8 November 2014|work=Yomiuri Shinbun|date=5 November 2014}}</ref>

== Legacy == A mural of him, removed from the old [[National Olympic Stadium (Tokyo)|National Olympic Stadium]] in 2014, was put on display at the new stadium in 2019.<ref>{{cite news|title=東京五輪向け壁画取り外し…建て替え国立競技場|url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/olympic/2020/20141105-OYT1T50114.html?from=ycont_top_txt|accessdate=8 November 2014|work=Yomiuri Shinbun|date=5 November 2014}}</ref>

{{Short description|Legendary sumo wrestler}} [[File:野見宿禰.jpg|thumb|200px|Nomi no Sukune by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]]]]
{{nihongo|'''Nomi no Sukune'''|野見 宿禰}} was a legendary figure in Japanese history, regarded as the founder of [[sumo]] wrestling.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book |url=https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/33014222 |title=Asashi Nihon rekishi jinbutsu jiten |date=1994 |publisher=Asahi Shinbunsha |isbn=4-02-340052-1 |at=藤原光能 |oclc=33014222}}</ref> He was the founder of the '''[[Haji clan|Haji]]''' no [[Muraji]] clan.<ref name=":3">{{Cite book |title=Nihon dai hyakka zensho |publisher=Shōgakkan |year=1989 |isbn=4-09-526001-7 |at=野見宿禰 |oclc=14970117}}</ref>
== Life == [[File:Nomi no Sukune Wrestling with Taima no Kehaya LACMA M.84.31.87.jpg|thumb|Nomi no Sukune wrestling with Taima no Kehaya (by [[Yoshitoshi]])]] Sukune is said to have lived during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] (29 BC – 70).<ref name="katano">{{cite web |title=The history of Kitano Jinja |url=http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |url-status=dead |publisher=Kitano Jinja |accessdate=2008-02-19 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080219104915/http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |archivedate=February 19, 2008}}</ref> Allegedly, in 23 B.C., the Emperor instructed Nomi no Sukune to deal with {{nihongo|Taima no Kehaya|当麻蹴速}} after he boasted that he was the strongest man "''under the heavens''". Nomi no Sukune engaged Taima no Kehaya in hand-to-hand combat and broke his ribs with one kick and his back with another, killing Taima no Kehaya.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Guttmann|first1=Allen|last2=Thompson|first2=Lee|title=Japanese Sports: A History|url=https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt|url-access=limited|date=2001|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|location=Honolulu, HI|isbn=9780824824143|page=[https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt/page/n24 14]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.essortment.com/wrestlingsumoh_rvqp.htm|title=Sumo wrestling history, the Ancient Japanese sport|publisher=Essortment|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> It was not modern sumo, but he is regarded as the creator of sumo.<ref>{{cite web |title=History of Sumo Wrestling |url=http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |accessdate=2008-02-19 |publisher=Houston Chinatown |archive-date=2008-03-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080320035028/http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |url-status=dead }}</ref><!-- He is also regarded as the human creator of [[jujutsu]].<ref name="aikido">{{cite web|url=http://www.aikido-shobukan.org/books/View.aspx?id=330|title=Canon of Judo "Principle and Technique"|author=Mifune, Kiyuzo|work=Aikido Shobukan Dojo|date=1959|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> -->
He was an ancestor of [[Sugawara no Michizane]],<ref name="katano" /> great-grandfather to Haji no Mino and was the founder of the Haji clan after his creation of ''[[haniwa]].''<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nomi no Sukune • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/|access-date=2021-09-01|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>
== Legacy == A mural of him, removed from the old [[National Olympic Stadium (Tokyo)|National Olympic Stadium]] in 2014, was put on display at the new stadium in 2019.<ref>{{cite news|title=東京五輪向け壁画取り外し…建て替え国立競技場|url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/olympic/2020/20141105-OYT1T50114.html?from=ycont_top_txt|accessdate=8 November 2014|work=Yomiuri Shinbun|date=5 November 2014}}</ref>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Legendary sumo wrestler}} [[File:野見宿禰.jpg|thumb|200px|Nomi no Sukune by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]]]]
{{nihongo|'''Nomi no Sukune'''|野見 宿禰}} was a legendary figure in Japanese history, regarded as the founder of [[sumo]] wrestling.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book |url=https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/33014222 |title=Asashi Nihon rekishi jinbutsu jiten |date=1994 |publisher=Asahi Shinbunsha |isbn=4-02-340052-1 |at=藤原光能 |oclc=33014222}}</ref> He was the founder of the '''[[Haji clan|Haji]]''' no [[Muraji]] clan.<ref name=":3">{{Cite book |title=Nihon dai hyakka zensho |publisher=Shōgakkan |year=1989 |isbn=4-09-526001-7 |at=野見宿禰 |oclc=14970117}}</ref>
== Life == [[File:Nomi no Sukune Wrestling with Taima no Kehaya LACMA M.84.31.87.jpg|thumb|Nomi no Sukune wrestling with Taima no Kehaya (by [[Yoshitoshi]])]] Sukune is said to have lived during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] (29 BC – 70).<ref name="katano">{{cite web |title=The history of Kitano Jinja |url=http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |url-status=dead |publisher=Kitano Jinja |accessdate=2008-02-19 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080219104915/http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |archivedate=February 19, 2008}}</ref> Allegedly, in 23 B.C., the Emperor instructed Nomi no Sukune to deal with {{nihongo|Taima no Kehaya|当麻蹴速}} after he boasted that he was the strongest man "''under the heavens''". Nomi no Sukune engaged Taima no Kehaya in hand-to-hand combat and broke his ribs with one kick and his back with another, killing Taima no Kehaya.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Guttmann|first1=Allen|last2=Thompson|first2=Lee|title=Japanese Sports: A History|url=https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt|url-access=limited|date=2001|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|location=Honolulu, HI|isbn=9780824824143|page=[https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt/page/n24 14]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.essortment.com/wrestlingsumoh_rvqp.htm|title=Sumo wrestling history, the Ancient Japanese sport|publisher=Essortment|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> It was not modern sumo, but he is regarded as the creator of sumo.<ref>{{cite web |title=History of Sumo Wrestling |url=http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |accessdate=2008-02-19 |publisher=Houston Chinatown |archive-date=2008-03-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080320035028/http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |url-status=dead }}</ref><!-- He is also regarded as the human creator of [[jujutsu]].<ref name="aikido">{{cite web|url=http://www.aikido-shobukan.org/books/View.aspx?id=330|title=Canon of Judo "Principle and Technique"|author=Mifune, Kiyuzo|work=Aikido Shobukan Dojo|date=1959|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> -->
He was an ancestor of [[Sugawara no Michizane]],<ref name="katano" /> great-grandfather to Haji no Mino and was the founder of the Haji clan after his creation of ''[[haniwa]].''<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nomi no Sukune • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/|access-date=2021-09-01|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>
== Legacy == A mural of him, removed from the old [[National Stadium (Tokyo)|National Stadium]] in 2014, was put on display at the new stadium in 2019.<ref>{{cite news|title=東京五輪向け壁画取り外し…建て替え国立競技場|url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/olympic/2020/20141105-OYT1T50114.html?from=ycont_top_txt|accessdate=8 November 2014|work=Yomiuri Shinbun|date=5 November 2014}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External links == * [https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/ Nomi no Sukune] - ''History of Japan Website.''

Nomi no Sukune engaged Taima no Kehaya in hand-to-hand combat and broke his ribs with one kick and his back with another, killing Taima no Kehaya.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Guttmann|first1=Allen|last2=Thompson|first2=Lee|title=Japanese Sports: A History|url=https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt|url-access=limited|date=2001|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|location=Honolulu, HI|isbn=9780824824143|page=[https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt/page/n24 14]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.essortment.com/wrestlingsumoh_rvqp.htm|title=Sumo wrestling history, the Ancient Japanese sport|publisher=Essortment|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> It was not modern sumo, but he is regarded as the creator of sumo.<ref>{{cite web |title=History of Sumo Wrestling |url=http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |accessdate=2008-02-19 |publisher=Houston Chinatown |archive-date=2008-03-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080320035028/http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |url-status=dead }}</ref><!-- He is also regarded as the human creator of [[jujutsu]].<ref name="aikido">{{cite web|url=http://www.aikido-shobukan.org/books/View.aspx?id=330|title=Canon of Judo "Principle and Technique"|author=Mifune, Kiyuzo|work=Aikido Shobukan Dojo|date=1959|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> -->
He was an ancestor of [[Sugawara no Michizane]],<ref name="katano" /> great-grandfather to Haji no Mino and was the founder of the Haji clan after his creation of ''[[haniwa]].''<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nomi no Sukune • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/|access-date=2021-09-01|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>
== Legacy == A mural of him, removed from the old [[National Stadium (Tokyo)|National Stadium]] in 2014, was put on display at the new stadium in 2019.<ref>{{cite news|title=東京五輪向け壁画取り外し…建て替え国立競技場|url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/olympic/2020/20141105-OYT1T50114.html?from=ycont_top_txt|accessdate=8 November 2014|work=Yomiuri Shinbun|date=5 November 2014}}</ref>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Legendary sumo wrestler}} [[File:野見宿禰.jpg|thumb|200px|Nomi no Sukune by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]]]]
{{nihongo|'''Nomi no Sukune'''|野見 宿禰}} was a legendary figure in Japanese history, regarded as the founder of [[sumo]] wrestling.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book |url=https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/33014222 |title=Asashi Nihon rekishi jinbutsu jiten |date=1994 |publisher=Asahi Shinbunsha |isbn=4-02-340052-1 |at=藤原光能 |oclc=33014222}}</ref> He was the founder of the '''[[Haji clan|Haji]]''' no [[Muraji]] clan.<ref name=":3">{{Cite book |title=Nihon dai hyakka zensho |publisher=Shōgakkan |year=1989 |isbn=4-09-526001-7 |at=野見宿禰 |oclc=14970117}}</ref>
== Life == [[File:Nomi no Sukune Wrestling with Taima no Kehaya LACMA M.84.31.87.jpg|thumb|Nomi no Sukune wrestling with Taima no Kehaya (by [[Yoshitoshi]])]] Sukune is said to have lived during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] (29 BC – 70).<ref name="katano">{{cite web |title=The history of Kitano Jinja |url=http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |url-status=dead |publisher=Kitano Jinja |accessdate=2008-02-19 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080219104915/http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |archivedate=February 19, 2008}}</ref> Allegedly, in 23 B.C., the Emperor instructed Nomi no Sukune to deal with {{nihongo|Taima no Kehaya|当麻蹴速}} after he boasted that he was the strongest man "''under the heavens''". Nomi no Sukune engaged Taima no Kehaya in hand-to-hand combat and broke his ribs with one kick and his back with another, killing Taima no Kehaya.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Guttmann|first1=Allen|last2=Thompson|first2=Lee|title=Japanese Sports: A History|url=https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt|url-access=limited|date=2001|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|location=Honolulu, HI|isbn=9780824824143|page=[https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt/page/n24 14]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.essortment.com/wrestlingsumoh_rvqp.htm|title=Sumo wrestling history, the Ancient Japanese sport|publisher=Essortment|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> It was not modern sumo, but he is regarded as the creator of sumo.<ref>{{cite web |title=History of Sumo Wrestling |url=http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |accessdate=2008-02-19 |publisher=Houston Chinatown |archive-date=2008-03-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080320035028/http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |url-status=dead }}</ref><!-- He is also regarded as the human creator of [[jujutsu]].<ref name="aikido">{{cite web|url=http://www.aikido-shobukan.org/books/View.aspx?id=330|title=Canon of Judo "Principle and Technique"|author=Mifune, Kiyuzo|work=Aikido Shobukan Dojo|date=1959|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> -->
He was an ancestor of [[Sugawara no Michizane]],<ref name="katano" /> great-grandfather to Haji no Mino and was the founder of the Haji clan after his creation of ''[[haniwa]].''<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nomi no Sukune • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/|access-date=2021-09-01|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>
== Legacy == A mural of him, removed from the old [[National Stadium (Tokyo)|National Stadium]] in 2014, was put on display at the new stadium in 2019.<ref>{{cite news|title=東京五輪向け壁画取り外し…建て替え国立競技場|url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/olympic/2020/20141105-OYT1T50114.html?from=ycont_top_txt|accessdate=8 November 2014|work=Yomiuri Shinbun|date=5 November 2014}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External links == * [https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/ Nomi no Sukune] - ''History of Japan Website.''

He was an ancestor of [[Sugawara no Michizane]],<ref name="katano" /> great-grandfather to Haji no Mino and was the founder of the Haji clan after his creation of ''[[haniwa]].''<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nomi no Sukune • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/|access-date=2021-09-01|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>
== Legacy == A mural of him, removed from the old [[National Stadium (Tokyo)|National Stadium]] in 2014, was put on display at the new stadium in 2019.<ref>{{cite news|title=東京五輪向け壁画取り外し…建て替え国立競技場|url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/olympic/2020/20141105-OYT1T50114.html?from=ycont_top_txt|accessdate=8 November 2014|work=Yomiuri Shinbun|date=5 November 2014}}</ref>

{{Short description|Legendary sumo wrestler}} [[File:野見宿禰.jpg|thumb|200px|Nomi no Sukune by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]]]]
{{nihongo|'''Nomi no Sukune'''|野見 宿禰}} was a legendary figure in Japanese history, regarded as the founder of [[sumo]] wrestling.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book |url=https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/33014222 |title=Asashi Nihon rekishi jinbutsu jiten |date=1994 |publisher=Asahi Shinbunsha |isbn=4-02-340052-1 |at=藤原光能 |oclc=33014222}}</ref> He was the founder of the '''[[Haji clan|Haji]]''' no [[Muraji]] clan.<ref name=":3">{{Cite book |title=Nihon dai hyakka zensho |publisher=Shōgakkan |year=1989 |isbn=4-09-526001-7 |at=野見宿禰 |oclc=14970117}}</ref>
== Life == [[File:Nomi no Sukune Wrestling with Taima no Kehaya LACMA M.84.31.87.jpg|thumb|Nomi no Sukune wrestling with Taima no Kehaya (by [[Yoshitoshi]])]] Sukune is said to have lived during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] (29 BC – 70).<ref name="katano">{{cite web |title=The history of Kitano Jinja |url=http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |url-status=dead |publisher=Kitano Jinja |accessdate=2008-02-19 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080219104915/http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |archivedate=February 19, 2008}}</ref> Allegedly, in 23 B.C., the Emperor instructed Nomi no Sukune to deal with {{nihongo|Taima no Kehaya|当麻蹴速}} after he boasted that he was the strongest man "''under the heavens''". Nomi no Sukune engaged Taima no Kehaya in hand-to-hand combat and broke his ribs with one kick and his back with another, killing Taima no Kehaya.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Guttmann|first1=Allen|last2=Thompson|first2=Lee|title=Japanese Sports: A History|url=https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt|url-access=limited|date=2001|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|location=Honolulu, HI|isbn=9780824824143|page=[https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt/page/n24 14]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.essortment.com/wrestlingsumoh_rvqp.htm|title=Sumo wrestling history, the Ancient Japanese sport|publisher=Essortment|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> It was not modern sumo, but he is regarded as the creator of sumo.<ref>{{cite web |title=History of Sumo Wrestling |url=http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |accessdate=2008-02-19 |publisher=Houston Chinatown |archive-date=2008-03-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080320035028/http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |url-status=dead }}</ref><!-- He is also regarded as the human creator of [[jujutsu]].<ref name="aikido">{{cite web|url=http://www.aikido-shobukan.org/books/View.aspx?id=330|title=Canon of Judo "Principle and Technique"|author=Mifune, Kiyuzo|work=Aikido Shobukan Dojo|date=1959|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> -->
He was an ancestor of [[Sugawara no Michizane]],<ref name="katano" /> great-grandfather to Haji no Mino and was the founder of the Haji clan after his creation of ''[[haniwa]].''<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nomi no Sukune • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/|access-date=2021-09-01|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>
== Legacy == A mural of him, removed from the old [[National Stadium (Tokyo)|National Stadium]] in 2014, was put on display at the new stadium in 2019.<ref>{{cite news|title=東京五輪向け壁画取り外し…建て替え国立競技場|url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/olympic/2020/20141105-OYT1T50114.html?from=ycont_top_txt|accessdate=8 November 2014|work=Yomiuri Shinbun|date=5 November 2014}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External links == * [https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/ Nomi no Sukune] - ''History of Japan Website.''

He was an ancestor of [[Sugawara no Michizane]],<ref name="katano" /> great-grandfather to Haji no Mino and was the founder of the Haji clan after his creation of ''[[haniwa]].''<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nomi no Sukune • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/|access-date=2021-09-01|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>
== Legacy == A mural of him, removed from the old [[National Stadium (Tokyo)|National Stadium]] in 2014, was put on display at the new stadium in 2019.<ref>{{cite news|title=東京五輪向け壁画取り外し…建て替え国立競技場|url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/olympic/2020/20141105-OYT1T50114.html?from=ycont_top_txt|accessdate=8 November 2014|work=Yomiuri Shinbun|date=5 November 2014}}</ref>

{{Short description|Legendary sumo wrestler}} [[File:野見宿禰.jpg|thumb|200px|Nomi no Sukune by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]]]]
{{nihongo|'''Nomi no Sukune'''|野見 宿禰}} was a legendary figure in Japanese history, regarded as the founder of [[sumo]] wrestling.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book |url=https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/33014222 |title=Asashi Nihon rekishi jinbutsu jiten |date=1994 |publisher=Asahi Shinbunsha |isbn=4-02-340052-1 |at=藤原光能 |oclc=33014222}}</ref> He was the founder of the '''[[Haji clan|Haji]]''' no [[Muraji]] clan.<ref name=":3">{{Cite book |title=Nihon dai hyakka zensho |publisher=Shōgakkan |year=1989 |isbn=4-09-526001-7 |at=野見宿禰 |oclc=14970117}}</ref>
== Life == [[File:Nomi no Sukune Wrestling with Taima no Kehaya LACMA M.84.31.87.jpg|thumb|Nomi no Sukune wrestling with Taima no Kehaya (by [[Yoshitoshi]])]] Sukune is said to have lived during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] (29 BC – 70).<ref name="katano">{{cite web |title=The history of Kitano Jinja |url=http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |url-status=dead |publisher=Kitano Jinja |accessdate=2008-02-19 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080219104915/http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |archivedate=February 19, 2008}}</ref> Allegedly, in 23 B.C., the Emperor instructed Nomi no Sukune to deal with {{nihongo|Taima no Kehaya|当麻蹴速}} after he boasted that he was the strongest man "''under the heavens''". Nomi no Sukune engaged Taima no Kehaya in hand-to-hand combat and broke his ribs with one kick and his back with another, killing Taima no Kehaya.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Guttmann|first1=Allen|last2=Thompson|first2=Lee|title=Japanese Sports: A History|url=https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt|url-access=limited|date=2001|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|location=Honolulu, HI|isbn=9780824824143|page=[https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt/page/n24 14]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.essortment.com/wrestlingsumoh_rvqp.htm|title=Sumo wrestling history, the Ancient Japanese sport|publisher=Essortment|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> It was not modern sumo, but he is regarded as the creator of sumo.<ref>{{cite web |title=History of Sumo Wrestling |url=http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |accessdate=2008-02-19 |publisher=Houston Chinatown |archive-date=2008-03-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080320035028/http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |url-status=dead }}</ref><!-- He is also regarded as the human creator of [[jujutsu]].<ref name="aikido">{{cite web|url=http://www.aikido-shobukan.org/books/View.aspx?id=330|title=Canon of Judo "Principle and Technique"|author=Mifune, Kiyuzo|work=Aikido Shobukan Dojo|date=1959|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> -->
He was an ancestor of [[Sugawara no Michizane]],<ref name="katano" /> great-grandfather to Haji no Mino and was the founder of the Haji clan after his creation of ''[[haniwa]].''<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nomi no Sukune • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/|access-date=2021-09-01|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>
== Legacy == A mural of him, removed from the old [[National Stadium (Tokyo)|National Stadium]] in 2014, was put on display at the new stadium in 2019.<ref>{{cite news|title=東京五輪向け壁画取り外し…建て替え国立競技場|url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/olympic/2020/20141105-OYT1T50114.html?from=ycont_top_txt|accessdate=8 November 2014|work=Yomiuri Shinbun|date=5 November 2014}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External links == * [https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/ Nomi no Sukune] - ''History of Japan Website.''

He was an ancestor of [[Sugawara no Michizane]],<ref name="katano" /> great-grandfather to Haji no Mino and was the founder of the Haji clan after his creation of ''[[haniwa]].''<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nomi no Sukune • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/|access-date=2021-09-01|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>
== Legacy == A mural of him, removed from the old [[National Stadium (Tokyo)|National Stadium]] in 2014, was put on display at the new stadium in 2019.<ref>{{cite news|title=東京五輪向け壁画取り外し…建て替え国立競技場|url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/olympic/2020/20141105-OYT1T50114.html?from=ycont_top_txt|accessdate=8 November 2014|work=Yomiuri Shinbun|date=5 November 2014}}</ref>

Nomi no Sukune engaged Taima no Kehaya in hand-to-hand combat and broke his ribs with one kick and his back with another, killing Taima no Kehaya.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Guttmann|first1=Allen|last2=Thompson|first2=Lee|title=Japanese Sports: A History|url=https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt|url-access=limited|date=2001|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|location=Honolulu, HI|isbn=9780824824143|page=[https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt/page/n24 14]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.essortment.com/wrestlingsumoh_rvqp.htm|title=Sumo wrestling history, the Ancient Japanese sport|publisher=Essortment|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> It was not modern sumo, but he is regarded as the creator of sumo.<ref>{{cite web |title=History of Sumo Wrestling |url=http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |accessdate=2008-02-19 |publisher=Houston Chinatown |archive-date=2008-03-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080320035028/http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |url-status=dead }}</ref><!-- He is also regarded as the human creator of [[jujutsu]].<ref name="aikido">{{cite web|url=http://www.aikido-shobukan.org/books/View.aspx?id=330|title=Canon of Judo "Principle and Technique"|author=Mifune, Kiyuzo|work=Aikido Shobukan Dojo|date=1959|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> -->

== Legacy == A mural of him, removed from the old [[National Stadium (Tokyo)|National Stadium]] in 2014, was put on display at the new stadium in 2019.<ref>{{cite news|title=東京五輪向け壁画取り外し…建て替え国立競技場|url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/olympic/2020/20141105-OYT1T50114.html?from=ycont_top_txt|accessdate=8 November 2014|work=Yomiuri Shinbun|date=5 November 2014}}</ref>

{{Short description|Legendary sumo wrestler}} [[File:野見宿禰.jpg|thumb|200px|Nomi no Sukune by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]]]]
{{nihongo|'''Nomi no Sukune'''|野見 宿禰}} was a legendary figure in Japanese history, regarded as the founder of [[sumo]] wrestling.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book |url=https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/33014222 |title=Asashi Nihon rekishi jinbutsu jiten |date=1994 |publisher=Asahi Shinbunsha |isbn=4-02-340052-1 |at=藤原光能 |oclc=33014222}}</ref> He was the founder of the '''[[Haji clan|Haji]]''' no [[Muraji]] clan.<ref name=":3">{{Cite book |title=Nihon dai hyakka zensho |publisher=Shōgakkan |year=1989 |isbn=4-09-526001-7 |at=野見宿禰 |oclc=14970117}}</ref>
== Life == [[File:Nomi no Sukune Wrestling with Taima no Kehaya LACMA M.84.31.87.jpg|thumb|Nomi no Sukune wrestling with Taima no Kehaya (by [[Yoshitoshi]])]] Sukune is said to have lived during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] (29 BC – 70).<ref name="katano">{{cite web |title=The history of Kitano Jinja |url=http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |url-status=dead |publisher=Kitano Jinja |accessdate=2008-02-19 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080219104915/http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |archivedate=February 19, 2008}}</ref> Allegedly, in 23 B.C., the Emperor instructed Nomi no Sukune to deal with {{nihongo|Taima no Kehaya|当麻蹴速}} after he boasted that he was the strongest man "''under the heavens''". Nomi no Sukune engaged Taima no Kehaya in hand-to-hand combat and broke his ribs with one kick and his back with another, killing Taima no Kehaya.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Guttmann|first1=Allen|last2=Thompson|first2=Lee|title=Japanese Sports: A History|url=https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt|url-access=limited|date=2001|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|location=Honolulu, HI|isbn=9780824824143|page=[https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt/page/n24 14]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.essortment.com/wrestlingsumoh_rvqp.htm|title=Sumo wrestling history, the Ancient Japanese sport|publisher=Essortment|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> It was not modern sumo, but he is regarded as the creator of sumo.<ref>{{cite web |title=History of Sumo Wrestling |url=http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |accessdate=2008-02-19 |publisher=Houston Chinatown |archive-date=2008-03-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080320035028/http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |url-status=dead }}</ref><!-- He is also regarded as the human creator of [[jujutsu]].<ref name="aikido">{{cite web|url=http://www.aikido-shobukan.org/books/View.aspx?id=330|title=Canon of Judo "Principle and Technique"|author=Mifune, Kiyuzo|work=Aikido Shobukan Dojo|date=1959|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> -->
He was an ancestor of [[Sugawara no Michizane]],<ref name="katano" /> great-grandfather to Haji no Mino and was the founder of the Haji clan after his creation of ''[[haniwa]].''<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nomi no Sukune • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/|access-date=2021-09-01|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>
== Legacy == A mural of him, removed from the old [[National Stadium (Tokyo)|National Stadium]] in 2014, was put on display at the new stadium in 2019.<ref>{{cite news|title=東京五輪向け壁画取り外し…建て替え国立競技場|url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/olympic/2020/20141105-OYT1T50114.html?from=ycont_top_txt|accessdate=8 November 2014|work=Yomiuri Shinbun|date=5 November 2014}}</ref>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Legendary sumo wrestler}} [[File:野見宿禰.jpg|thumb|200px|Nomi no Sukune by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]]]]
{{nihongo|'''Nomi no Sukune'''|野見 宿禰}} was a legendary figure in Japanese history, regarded as the founder of [[sumo]] wrestling.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book |url=https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/33014222 |title=Asashi Nihon rekishi jinbutsu jiten |date=1994 |publisher=Asahi Shinbunsha |isbn=4-02-340052-1 |at=藤原光能 |oclc=33014222}}</ref> He was the founder of the '''[[Haji clan|Haji]]''' no [[Muraji]] clan.<ref name=":3">{{Cite book |title=Nihon dai hyakka zensho |publisher=Shōgakkan |year=1989 |isbn=4-09-526001-7 |at=野見宿禰 |oclc=14970117}}</ref>
== Life == [[File:Nomi no Sukune Wrestling with Taima no Kehaya LACMA M.84.31.87.jpg|thumb|Nomi no Sukune wrestling with Taima no Kehaya (by [[Yoshitoshi]])]] Sukune is said to have lived during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] (29 BC – 70).<ref name="katano">{{cite web |title=The history of Kitano Jinja |url=http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |url-status=dead |publisher=Kitano Jinja |accessdate=2008-02-19 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080219104915/http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |archivedate=February 19, 2008}}</ref> Allegedly, in 23 B.C., the Emperor instructed Nomi no Sukune to deal with {{nihongo|Taima no Kehaya|当麻蹴速}} after he boasted that he was the strongest man "''under the heavens''". Nomi no Sukune engaged Taima no Kehaya in hand-to-hand combat and broke his ribs with one kick and his back with another, killing Taima no Kehaya.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Guttmann|first1=Allen|last2=Thompson|first2=Lee|title=Japanese Sports: A History|url=https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt|url-access=limited|date=2001|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|location=Honolulu, HI|isbn=9780824824143|page=[https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt/page/n24 14]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.essortment.com/wrestlingsumoh_rvqp.htm|title=Sumo wrestling history, the Ancient Japanese sport|publisher=Essortment|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> It was not modern sumo, but he is regarded as the creator of sumo.<ref>{{cite web |title=History of Sumo Wrestling |url=http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |accessdate=2008-02-19 |publisher=Houston Chinatown |archive-date=2008-03-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080320035028/http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |url-status=dead }}</ref><!-- He is also regarded as the human creator of [[jujutsu]].<ref name="aikido">{{cite web|url=http://www.aikido-shobukan.org/books/View.aspx?id=330|title=Canon of Judo "Principle and Technique"|author=Mifune, Kiyuzo|work=Aikido Shobukan Dojo|date=1959|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> -->
He was an ancestor of [[Sugawara no Michizane]],<ref name="katano" /> great-grandfather to Haji no Mino and was the founder of the Haji clan after his creation of ''[[haniwa]].''<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nomi no Sukune • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/|access-date=2021-09-01|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>
== Legacy == A mural of him, removed from the old [[National Stadium (Tokyo)|National Stadium]] in 2014, was put on display at the new stadium in 2019.<ref>{{cite news|title=東京五輪向け壁画取り外し…建て替え国立競技場|url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/olympic/2020/20141105-OYT1T50114.html?from=ycont_top_txt|accessdate=8 November 2014|work=Yomiuri Shinbun|date=5 November 2014}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External links == * [https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/ Nomi no Sukune] - ''History of Japan Website.''

He was an ancestor of [[Sugawara no Michizane]],<ref name="katano" /> great-grandfather to Haji no Mino and was the founder of the Haji clan after his creation of ''[[haniwa]].''<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nomi no Sukune • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/|access-date=2021-09-01|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>

{{Short description|Legendary sumo wrestler}} [[File:野見宿禰.jpg|thumb|200px|Nomi no Sukune by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]]]]
{{nihongo|'''Nomi no Sukune'''|野見 宿禰}} was a legendary figure in Japanese history, regarded as the founder of [[sumo]] wrestling.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book |url=https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/33014222 |title=Asashi Nihon rekishi jinbutsu jiten |date=1994 |publisher=Asahi Shinbunsha |isbn=4-02-340052-1 |at=藤原光能 |oclc=33014222}}</ref> He was the founder of the '''[[Haji clan|Haji]]''' no [[Muraji]] clan.<ref name=":3">{{Cite book |title=Nihon dai hyakka zensho |publisher=Shōgakkan |year=1989 |isbn=4-09-526001-7 |at=野見宿禰 |oclc=14970117}}</ref>
== Life == [[File:Nomi no Sukune Wrestling with Taima no Kehaya LACMA M.84.31.87.jpg|thumb|Nomi no Sukune wrestling with Taima no Kehaya (by [[Yoshitoshi]])]] Sukune is said to have lived during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] (29 BC – 70).<ref name="katano">{{cite web |title=The history of Kitano Jinja |url=http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |url-status=dead |publisher=Kitano Jinja |accessdate=2008-02-19 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080219104915/http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |archivedate=February 19, 2008}}</ref> Allegedly, in 23 B.C., the Emperor instructed Nomi no Sukune to deal with {{nihongo|Taima no Kehaya|当麻蹴速}} after he boasted that he was the strongest man "''under the heavens''". Nomi no Sukune engaged Taima no Kehaya in hand-to-hand combat and broke his ribs with one kick and his back with another, killing Taima no Kehaya.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Guttmann|first1=Allen|last2=Thompson|first2=Lee|title=Japanese Sports: A History|url=https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt|url-access=limited|date=2001|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|location=Honolulu, HI|isbn=9780824824143|page=[https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt/page/n24 14]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.essortment.com/wrestlingsumoh_rvqp.htm|title=Sumo wrestling history, the Ancient Japanese sport|publisher=Essortment|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> It was not modern sumo, but he is regarded as the creator of sumo.<ref>{{cite web |title=History of Sumo Wrestling |url=http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |accessdate=2008-02-19 |publisher=Houston Chinatown |archive-date=2008-03-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080320035028/http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |url-status=dead }}</ref><!-- He is also regarded as the human creator of [[jujutsu]].<ref name="aikido">{{cite web|url=http://www.aikido-shobukan.org/books/View.aspx?id=330|title=Canon of Judo "Principle and Technique"|author=Mifune, Kiyuzo|work=Aikido Shobukan Dojo|date=1959|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> -->
He was an ancestor of [[Sugawara no Michizane]],<ref name="katano" /> great-grandfather to Haji no Mino and was the founder of the Haji clan after his creation of ''[[haniwa]].''<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nomi no Sukune • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/|access-date=2021-09-01|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>
== Legacy == A mural of him, removed from the old [[National Stadium (Tokyo)|National Stadium]] in 2014, was put on display at the new stadium in 2019.<ref>{{cite news|title=東京五輪向け壁画取り外し…建て替え国立競技場|url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/olympic/2020/20141105-OYT1T50114.html?from=ycont_top_txt|accessdate=8 November 2014|work=Yomiuri Shinbun|date=5 November 2014}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External links == * [https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/ Nomi no Sukune] - ''History of Japan Website.''

== Life == [[File:Nomi no Sukune Wrestling with Taima no Kehaya LACMA M.84.31.87.jpg|thumb|Nomi no Sukune wrestling with Taima no Kehaya (by [[Yoshitoshi]])]] Sukune is said to have lived during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] (29 BC – 70).<ref name="katano">{{cite web |title=The history of Kitano Jinja |url=http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |url-status=dead |publisher=Kitano Jinja |accessdate=2008-02-19 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080219104915/http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |archivedate=February 19, 2008}}</ref> Allegedly, in 23 B.C., the Emperor instructed Nomi no Sukune to deal with {{nihongo|Taima no Kehaya|当麻蹴速}} after he boasted that he was the strongest man "''under the heavens''". Nomi no Sukune engaged Taima no Kehaya in hand-to-hand combat and broke his ribs with one kick and his back with another, killing Taima no Kehaya.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Guttmann|first1=Allen|last2=Thompson|first2=Lee|title=Japanese Sports: A History|url=https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt|url-access=limited|date=2001|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|location=Honolulu, HI|isbn=9780824824143|page=[https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt/page/n24 14]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.essortment.com/wrestlingsumoh_rvqp.htm|title=Sumo wrestling history, the Ancient Japanese sport|publisher=Essortment|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> It was not modern sumo, but he is regarded as the creator of sumo.<ref>{{cite web |title=History of Sumo Wrestling |url=http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |accessdate=2008-02-19 |publisher=Houston Chinatown |archive-date=2008-03-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080320035028/http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |url-status=dead }}</ref><!-- He is also regarded as the human creator of [[jujutsu]].<ref name="aikido">{{cite web|url=http://www.aikido-shobukan.org/books/View.aspx?id=330|title=Canon of Judo "Principle and Technique"|author=Mifune, Kiyuzo|work=Aikido Shobukan Dojo|date=1959|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> -->
He was an ancestor of [[Sugawara no Michizane]],<ref name="katano" /> great-grandfather to Haji no Mino and was the founder of the Haji clan after his creation of ''[[haniwa]].''<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nomi no Sukune • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/|access-date=2021-09-01|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>

== Life == [[File:Nomi no Sukune Wrestling with Taima no Kehaya LACMA M.84.31.87.jpg|thumb|Nomi no Sukune wrestling with Taima no Kehaya (by [[Yoshitoshi]])]] Sukune is said to have lived during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] (29 BC – 70).<ref name="katano">{{cite web |title=The history of Kitano Jinja |url=http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |url-status=dead |publisher=Kitano Jinja |accessdate=2008-02-19 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080219104915/http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |archivedate=February 19, 2008}}</ref> Allegedly, in 23 B.C., the Emperor instructed Nomi no Sukune to deal with {{nihongo|Taima no Kehaya|当麻蹴速}} after he boasted that he was the strongest man "''under the heavens''". Nomi no Sukune engaged Taima no Kehaya in hand-to-hand combat and broke his ribs with one kick and his back with another, killing Taima no Kehaya.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Guttmann|first1=Allen|last2=Thompson|first2=Lee|title=Japanese Sports: A History|url=https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt|url-access=limited|date=2001|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|location=Honolulu, HI|isbn=9780824824143|page=[https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt/page/n24 14]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.essortment.com/wrestlingsumoh_rvqp.htm|title=Sumo wrestling history, the Ancient Japanese sport|publisher=Essortment|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> It was not modern sumo, but he is regarded as the creator of sumo.<ref>{{cite web |title=History of Sumo Wrestling |url=http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |accessdate=2008-02-19 |publisher=Houston Chinatown |archive-date=2008-03-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080320035028/http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |url-status=dead }}</ref><!-- He is also regarded as the human creator of [[jujutsu]].<ref name="aikido">{{cite web|url=http://www.aikido-shobukan.org/books/View.aspx?id=330|title=Canon of Judo "Principle and Technique"|author=Mifune, Kiyuzo|work=Aikido Shobukan Dojo|date=1959|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> -->

{{Short description|Legendary sumo wrestler}} [[File:野見宿禰.jpg|thumb|200px|Nomi no Sukune by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]]]]
{{nihongo|'''Nomi no Sukune'''|野見 宿禰}} was a legendary figure in Japanese history, regarded as the founder of [[sumo]] wrestling.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book |url=https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/33014222 |title=Asashi Nihon rekishi jinbutsu jiten |date=1994 |publisher=Asahi Shinbunsha |isbn=4-02-340052-1 |at=藤原光能 |oclc=33014222}}</ref> He was the founder of the '''[[Haji clan|Haji]]''' no [[Muraji]] clan.<ref name=":3">{{Cite book |title=Nihon dai hyakka zensho |publisher=Shōgakkan |year=1989 |isbn=4-09-526001-7 |at=野見宿禰 |oclc=14970117}}</ref>
== Life == [[File:Nomi no Sukune Wrestling with Taima no Kehaya LACMA M.84.31.87.jpg|thumb|Nomi no Sukune wrestling with Taima no Kehaya (by [[Yoshitoshi]])]] Sukune is said to have lived during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] (29 BC – 70).<ref name="katano">{{cite web |title=The history of Kitano Jinja |url=http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |url-status=dead |publisher=Kitano Jinja |accessdate=2008-02-19 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080219104915/http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |archivedate=February 19, 2008}}</ref> Allegedly, in 23 B.C., the Emperor instructed Nomi no Sukune to deal with {{nihongo|Taima no Kehaya|当麻蹴速}} after he boasted that he was the strongest man "''under the heavens''". Nomi no Sukune engaged Taima no Kehaya in hand-to-hand combat and broke his ribs with one kick and his back with another, killing Taima no Kehaya.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Guttmann|first1=Allen|last2=Thompson|first2=Lee|title=Japanese Sports: A History|url=https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt|url-access=limited|date=2001|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|location=Honolulu, HI|isbn=9780824824143|page=[https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt/page/n24 14]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.essortment.com/wrestlingsumoh_rvqp.htm|title=Sumo wrestling history, the Ancient Japanese sport|publisher=Essortment|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> It was not modern sumo, but he is regarded as the creator of sumo.<ref>{{cite web |title=History of Sumo Wrestling |url=http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |accessdate=2008-02-19 |publisher=Houston Chinatown |archive-date=2008-03-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080320035028/http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |url-status=dead }}</ref><!-- He is also regarded as the human creator of [[jujutsu]].<ref name="aikido">{{cite web|url=http://www.aikido-shobukan.org/books/View.aspx?id=330|title=Canon of Judo "Principle and Technique"|author=Mifune, Kiyuzo|work=Aikido Shobukan Dojo|date=1959|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> -->
He was an ancestor of [[Sugawara no Michizane]],<ref name="katano" /> great-grandfather to Haji no Mino and was the founder of the Haji clan after his creation of ''[[haniwa]].''<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nomi no Sukune • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/|access-date=2021-09-01|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>
== Legacy == A mural of him, removed from the old [[National Stadium (Tokyo)|National Stadium]] in 2014, was put on display at the new stadium in 2019.<ref>{{cite news|title=東京五輪向け壁画取り外し…建て替え国立競技場|url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/olympic/2020/20141105-OYT1T50114.html?from=ycont_top_txt|accessdate=8 November 2014|work=Yomiuri Shinbun|date=5 November 2014}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External links == * [https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/ Nomi no Sukune] - ''History of Japan Website.''

{{Short description|Legendary sumo wrestler}} [[File:野見宿禰.jpg|thumb|200px|Nomi no Sukune by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]]]]
{{nihongo|'''Nomi no Sukune'''|野見 宿禰}} was a legendary figure in Japanese history, regarded as the founder of [[sumo]] wrestling.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book |url=https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/33014222 |title=Asashi Nihon rekishi jinbutsu jiten |date=1994 |publisher=Asahi Shinbunsha |isbn=4-02-340052-1 |at=藤原光能 |oclc=33014222}}</ref> He was the founder of the '''[[Haji clan|Haji]]''' no [[Muraji]] clan.<ref name=":3">{{Cite book |title=Nihon dai hyakka zensho |publisher=Shōgakkan |year=1989 |isbn=4-09-526001-7 |at=野見宿禰 |oclc=14970117}}</ref>
== Life == [[File:Nomi no Sukune Wrestling with Taima no Kehaya LACMA M.84.31.87.jpg|thumb|Nomi no Sukune wrestling with Taima no Kehaya (by [[Yoshitoshi]])]] Sukune is said to have lived during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] (29 BC – 70).<ref name="katano">{{cite web |title=The history of Kitano Jinja |url=http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |url-status=dead |publisher=Kitano Jinja |accessdate=2008-02-19 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080219104915/http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |archivedate=February 19, 2008}}</ref> Allegedly, in 23 B.C., the Emperor instructed Nomi no Sukune to deal with {{nihongo|Taima no Kehaya|当麻蹴速}} after he boasted that he was the strongest man "''under the heavens''". Nomi no Sukune engaged Taima no Kehaya in hand-to-hand combat and broke his ribs with one kick and his back with another, killing Taima no Kehaya.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Guttmann|first1=Allen|last2=Thompson|first2=Lee|title=Japanese Sports: A History|url=https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt|url-access=limited|date=2001|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|location=Honolulu, HI|isbn=9780824824143|page=[https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt/page/n24 14]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.essortment.com/wrestlingsumoh_rvqp.htm|title=Sumo wrestling history, the Ancient Japanese sport|publisher=Essortment|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> It was not modern sumo, but he is regarded as the creator of sumo.<ref>{{cite web |title=History of Sumo Wrestling |url=http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |accessdate=2008-02-19 |publisher=Houston Chinatown |archive-date=2008-03-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080320035028/http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |url-status=dead }}</ref><!-- He is also regarded as the human creator of [[jujutsu]].<ref name="aikido">{{cite web|url=http://www.aikido-shobukan.org/books/View.aspx?id=330|title=Canon of Judo "Principle and Technique"|author=Mifune, Kiyuzo|work=Aikido Shobukan Dojo|date=1959|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> -->
He was an ancestor of [[Sugawara no Michizane]],<ref name="katano" /> great-grandfather to Haji no Mino and was the founder of the Haji clan after his creation of ''[[haniwa]].''<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nomi no Sukune • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/|access-date=2021-09-01|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>
== Legacy == A mural of him, removed from the old [[National Stadium (Tokyo)|National Stadium]] in 2014, was put on display at the new stadium in 2019.<ref>{{cite news|title=東京五輪向け壁画取り外し…建て替え国立競技場|url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/olympic/2020/20141105-OYT1T50114.html?from=ycont_top_txt|accessdate=8 November 2014|work=Yomiuri Shinbun|date=5 November 2014}}</ref>

{{Short description|Legendary sumo wrestler}} [[File:野見宿禰.jpg|thumb|200px|Nomi no Sukune by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]]]]
{{nihongo|'''Nomi no Sukune'''|野見 宿禰}} was a legendary figure in Japanese history, regarded as the founder of [[sumo]] wrestling.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book |url=https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/33014222 |title=Asashi Nihon rekishi jinbutsu jiten |date=1994 |publisher=Asahi Shinbunsha |isbn=4-02-340052-1 |at=藤原光能 |oclc=33014222}}</ref> He was the founder of the '''[[Haji clan|Haji]]''' no [[Muraji]] clan.<ref name=":3">{{Cite book |title=Nihon dai hyakka zensho |publisher=Shōgakkan |year=1989 |isbn=4-09-526001-7 |at=野見宿禰 |oclc=14970117}}</ref>
== Life == [[File:Nomi no Sukune Wrestling with Taima no Kehaya LACMA M.84.31.87.jpg|thumb|Nomi no Sukune wrestling with Taima no Kehaya (by [[Yoshitoshi]])]] Sukune is said to have lived during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] (29 BC – 70).<ref name="katano">{{cite web |title=The history of Kitano Jinja |url=http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |url-status=dead |publisher=Kitano Jinja |accessdate=2008-02-19 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080219104915/http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |archivedate=February 19, 2008}}</ref> Allegedly, in 23 B.C., the Emperor instructed Nomi no Sukune to deal with {{nihongo|Taima no Kehaya|当麻蹴速}} after he boasted that he was the strongest man "''under the heavens''". Nomi no Sukune engaged Taima no Kehaya in hand-to-hand combat and broke his ribs with one kick and his back with another, killing Taima no Kehaya.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Guttmann|first1=Allen|last2=Thompson|first2=Lee|title=Japanese Sports: A History|url=https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt|url-access=limited|date=2001|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|location=Honolulu, HI|isbn=9780824824143|page=[https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt/page/n24 14]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.essortment.com/wrestlingsumoh_rvqp.htm|title=Sumo wrestling history, the Ancient Japanese sport|publisher=Essortment|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> It was not modern sumo, but he is regarded as the creator of sumo.<ref>{{cite web |title=History of Sumo Wrestling |url=http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |accessdate=2008-02-19 |publisher=Houston Chinatown |archive-date=2008-03-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080320035028/http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |url-status=dead }}</ref><!-- He is also regarded as the human creator of [[jujutsu]].<ref name="aikido">{{cite web|url=http://www.aikido-shobukan.org/books/View.aspx?id=330|title=Canon of Judo "Principle and Technique"|author=Mifune, Kiyuzo|work=Aikido Shobukan Dojo|date=1959|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> -->
He was an ancestor of [[Sugawara no Michizane]],<ref name="katano" /> great-grandfather to Haji no Mino and was the founder of the Haji clan after his creation of ''[[haniwa]].''<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nomi no Sukune • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/|access-date=2021-09-01|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>
== Legacy == A mural of him, removed from the old [[National Stadium (Tokyo)|National Stadium]] in 2014, was put on display at the new stadium in 2019.<ref>{{cite news|title=東京五輪向け壁画取り外し…建て替え国立競技場|url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/olympic/2020/20141105-OYT1T50114.html?from=ycont_top_txt|accessdate=8 November 2014|work=Yomiuri Shinbun|date=5 November 2014}}</ref>

{{Short description|Legendary sumo wrestler}} [[File:野見宿禰.jpg|thumb|200px|Nomi no Sukune by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]]]]
{{nihongo|'''Nomi no Sukune'''|野見 宿禰}} was a legendary figure in Japanese history, regarded as the founder of [[sumo]] wrestling.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book |url=https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/33014222 |title=Asashi Nihon rekishi jinbutsu jiten |date=1994 |publisher=Asahi Shinbunsha |isbn=4-02-340052-1 |at=藤原光能 |oclc=33014222}}</ref> He was the founder of the '''[[Haji clan|Haji]]''' no [[Muraji]] clan.<ref name=":3">{{Cite book |title=Nihon dai hyakka zensho |publisher=Shōgakkan |year=1989 |isbn=4-09-526001-7 |at=野見宿禰 |oclc=14970117}}</ref>
== Life == [[File:Nomi no Sukune Wrestling with Taima no Kehaya LACMA M.84.31.87.jpg|thumb|Nomi no Sukune wrestling with Taima no Kehaya (by [[Yoshitoshi]])]] Sukune is said to have lived during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] (29 BC – 70).<ref name="katano">{{cite web |title=The history of Kitano Jinja |url=http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |url-status=dead |publisher=Kitano Jinja |accessdate=2008-02-19 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080219104915/http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |archivedate=February 19, 2008}}</ref> Allegedly, in 23 B.C., the Emperor instructed Nomi no Sukune to deal with {{nihongo|Taima no Kehaya|当麻蹴速}} after he boasted that he was the strongest man "''under the heavens''". Nomi no Sukune engaged Taima no Kehaya in hand-to-hand combat and broke his ribs with one kick and his back with another, killing Taima no Kehaya.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Guttmann|first1=Allen|last2=Thompson|first2=Lee|title=Japanese Sports: A History|url=https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt|url-access=limited|date=2001|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|location=Honolulu, HI|isbn=9780824824143|page=[https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt/page/n24 14]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.essortment.com/wrestlingsumoh_rvqp.htm|title=Sumo wrestling history, the Ancient Japanese sport|publisher=Essortment|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> It was not modern sumo, but he is regarded as the creator of sumo.<ref>{{cite web |title=History of Sumo Wrestling |url=http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |accessdate=2008-02-19 |publisher=Houston Chinatown |archive-date=2008-03-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080320035028/http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |url-status=dead }}</ref><!-- He is also regarded as the human creator of [[jujutsu]].<ref name="aikido">{{cite web|url=http://www.aikido-shobukan.org/books/View.aspx?id=330|title=Canon of Judo "Principle and Technique"|author=Mifune, Kiyuzo|work=Aikido Shobukan Dojo|date=1959|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> -->
He was an ancestor of [[Sugawara no Michizane]],<ref name="katano" /> great-grandfather to Haji no Mino and was the founder of the Haji clan after his creation of ''[[haniwa]].''<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nomi no Sukune • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/|access-date=2021-09-01|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>
== Legacy == A mural of him, removed from the old [[National Stadium (Tokyo)|National Stadium]] in 2014, was put on display at the new stadium in 2019.<ref>{{cite news|title=東京五輪向け壁画取り外し…建て替え国立競技場|url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/olympic/2020/20141105-OYT1T50114.html?from=ycont_top_txt|accessdate=8 November 2014|work=Yomiuri Shinbun|date=5 November 2014}}</ref>

{{Short description|Legendary sumo wrestler}} [[File:野見宿禰.jpg|thumb|200px|Nomi no Sukune by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]]]]
{{nihongo|'''Nomi no Sukune'''|野見 宿禰}} was a legendary figure in Japanese history, regarded as the founder of [[sumo]] wrestling.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book |url=https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/33014222 |title=Asashi Nihon rekishi jinbutsu jiten |date=1994 |publisher=Asahi Shinbunsha |isbn=4-02-340052-1 |at=藤原光能 |oclc=33014222}}</ref> He was the founder of the '''[[Haji clan|Haji]]''' no [[Muraji]] clan.<ref name=":3">{{Cite book |title=Nihon dai hyakka zensho |publisher=Shōgakkan |year=1989 |isbn=4-09-526001-7 |at=野見宿禰 |oclc=14970117}}</ref>
== Life == [[File:Nomi no Sukune Wrestling with Taima no Kehaya LACMA M.84.31.87.jpg|thumb|Nomi no Sukune wrestling with Taima no Kehaya (by [[Yoshitoshi]])]] Sukune is said to have lived during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] (29 BC – 70).<ref name="katano">{{cite web |title=The history of Kitano Jinja |url=http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |url-status=dead |publisher=Kitano Jinja |accessdate=2008-02-19 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080219104915/http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |archivedate=February 19, 2008}}</ref> Allegedly, in 23 B.C., the Emperor instructed Nomi no Sukune to deal with {{nihongo|Taima no Kehaya|当麻蹴速}} after he boasted that he was the strongest man "''under the heavens''". Nomi no Sukune engaged Taima no Kehaya in hand-to-hand combat and broke his ribs with one kick and his back with another, killing Taima no Kehaya.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Guttmann|first1=Allen|last2=Thompson|first2=Lee|title=Japanese Sports: A History|url=https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt|url-access=limited|date=2001|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|location=Honolulu, HI|isbn=9780824824143|page=[https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt/page/n24 14]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.essortment.com/wrestlingsumoh_rvqp.htm|title=Sumo wrestling history, the Ancient Japanese sport|publisher=Essortment|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> It was not modern sumo, but he is regarded as the creator of sumo.<ref>{{cite web |title=History of Sumo Wrestling |url=http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |accessdate=2008-02-19 |publisher=Houston Chinatown |archive-date=2008-03-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080320035028/http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |url-status=dead }}</ref><!-- He is also regarded as the human creator of [[jujutsu]].<ref name="aikido">{{cite web|url=http://www.aikido-shobukan.org/books/View.aspx?id=330|title=Canon of Judo "Principle and Technique"|author=Mifune, Kiyuzo|work=Aikido Shobukan Dojo|date=1959|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> -->
He was an ancestor of [[Sugawara no Michizane]],<ref name="katano" /> great-grandfather to Haji no Mino and was the founder of the Haji clan after his creation of ''[[haniwa]].''<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nomi no Sukune • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/|access-date=2021-09-01|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>
== Legacy == A mural of him, removed from the old [[National Stadium (Tokyo)|National Stadium]] in 2014, was put on display at the new stadium in 2019.<ref>{{cite news|title=東京五輪向け壁画取り外し…建て替え国立競技場|url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/olympic/2020/20141105-OYT1T50114.html?from=ycont_top_txt|accessdate=8 November 2014|work=Yomiuri Shinbun|date=5 November 2014}}</ref>

{{Short description|Legendary sumo wrestler}} [[File:野見宿禰.jpg|thumb|200px|Nomi no Sukune by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]]]]
{{nihongo|'''Nomi no Sukune'''|野見 宿禰}} was a legendary figure in Japanese history, regarded as the founder of [[sumo]] wrestling.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book |url=https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/33014222 |title=Asashi Nihon rekishi jinbutsu jiten |date=1994 |publisher=Asahi Shinbunsha |isbn=4-02-340052-1 |at=藤原光能 |oclc=33014222}}</ref> He was the founder of the '''[[Haji clan|Haji]]''' no [[Muraji]] clan.<ref name=":3">{{Cite book |title=Nihon dai hyakka zensho |publisher=Shōgakkan |year=1989 |isbn=4-09-526001-7 |at=野見宿禰 |oclc=14970117}}</ref>
== Life == [[File:Nomi no Sukune Wrestling with Taima no Kehaya LACMA M.84.31.87.jpg|thumb|Nomi no Sukune wrestling with Taima no Kehaya (by [[Yoshitoshi]])]] Sukune is said to have lived during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] (29 BC – 70).<ref name="katano">{{cite web |title=The history of Kitano Jinja |url=http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |url-status=dead |publisher=Kitano Jinja |accessdate=2008-02-19 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080219104915/http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |archivedate=February 19, 2008}}</ref> Allegedly, in 23 B.C., the Emperor instructed Nomi no Sukune to deal with {{nihongo|Taima no Kehaya|当麻蹴速}} after he boasted that he was the strongest man "''under the heavens''". Nomi no Sukune engaged Taima no Kehaya in hand-to-hand combat and broke his ribs with one kick and his back with another, killing Taima no Kehaya.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Guttmann|first1=Allen|last2=Thompson|first2=Lee|title=Japanese Sports: A History|url=https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt|url-access=limited|date=2001|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|location=Honolulu, HI|isbn=9780824824143|page=[https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt/page/n24 14]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.essortment.com/wrestlingsumoh_rvqp.htm|title=Sumo wrestling history, the Ancient Japanese sport|publisher=Essortment|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> It was not modern sumo, but he is regarded as the creator of sumo.<ref>{{cite web |title=History of Sumo Wrestling |url=http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |accessdate=2008-02-19 |publisher=Houston Chinatown |archive-date=2008-03-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080320035028/http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |url-status=dead }}</ref><!-- He is also regarded as the human creator of [[jujutsu]].<ref name="aikido">{{cite web|url=http://www.aikido-shobukan.org/books/View.aspx?id=330|title=Canon of Judo "Principle and Technique"|author=Mifune, Kiyuzo|work=Aikido Shobukan Dojo|date=1959|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> -->
He was an ancestor of [[Sugawara no Michizane]],<ref name="katano" /> great-grandfather to Haji no Mino and was the founder of the Haji clan after his creation of ''[[haniwa]].''<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nomi no Sukune • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/|access-date=2021-09-01|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>
== Legacy == A mural of him, removed from the old [[National Stadium (Tokyo)|National Stadium]] in 2014, was put on display at the new stadium in 2019.<ref>{{cite news|title=東京五輪向け壁画取り外し…建て替え国立競技場|url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/olympic/2020/20141105-OYT1T50114.html?from=ycont_top_txt|accessdate=8 November 2014|work=Yomiuri Shinbun|date=5 November 2014}}</ref>

{{Short description|Legendary sumo wrestler}} [[File:野見宿禰.jpg|thumb|200px|Nomi no Sukune by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]]]]
{{nihongo|'''Nomi no Sukune'''|野見 宿禰}} was a legendary figure in Japanese history, regarded as the founder of [[sumo]] wrestling.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book |url=https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/33014222 |title=Asashi Nihon rekishi jinbutsu jiten |date=1994 |publisher=Asahi Shinbunsha |isbn=4-02-340052-1 |at=藤原光能 |oclc=33014222}}</ref> He was the founder of the '''[[Haji clan|Haji]]''' no [[Muraji]] clan.<ref name=":3">{{Cite book |title=Nihon dai hyakka zensho |publisher=Shōgakkan |year=1989 |isbn=4-09-526001-7 |at=野見宿禰 |oclc=14970117}}</ref>
== Life == [[File:Nomi no Sukune Wrestling with Taima no Kehaya LACMA M.84.31.87.jpg|thumb|Nomi no Sukune wrestling with Taima no Kehaya (by [[Yoshitoshi]])]] Sukune is said to have lived during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] (29 BC – 70).<ref name="katano">{{cite web |title=The history of Kitano Jinja |url=http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |url-status=dead |publisher=Kitano Jinja |accessdate=2008-02-19 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080219104915/http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |archivedate=February 19, 2008}}</ref> Allegedly, in 23 B.C., the Emperor instructed Nomi no Sukune to deal with {{nihongo|Taima no Kehaya|当麻蹴速}} after he boasted that he was the strongest man "''under the heavens''". Nomi no Sukune engaged Taima no Kehaya in hand-to-hand combat and broke his ribs with one kick and his back with another, killing Taima no Kehaya.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Guttmann|first1=Allen|last2=Thompson|first2=Lee|title=Japanese Sports: A History|url=https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt|url-access=limited|date=2001|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|location=Honolulu, HI|isbn=9780824824143|page=[https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt/page/n24 14]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.essortment.com/wrestlingsumoh_rvqp.htm|title=Sumo wrestling history, the Ancient Japanese sport|publisher=Essortment|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> It was not modern sumo, but he is regarded as the creator of sumo.<ref>{{cite web |title=History of Sumo Wrestling |url=http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |accessdate=2008-02-19 |publisher=Houston Chinatown |archive-date=2008-03-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080320035028/http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |url-status=dead }}</ref><!-- He is also regarded as the human creator of [[jujutsu]].<ref name="aikido">{{cite web|url=http://www.aikido-shobukan.org/books/View.aspx?id=330|title=Canon of Judo "Principle and Technique"|author=Mifune, Kiyuzo|work=Aikido Shobukan Dojo|date=1959|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> -->
He was an ancestor of [[Sugawara no Michizane]],<ref name="katano" /> great-grandfather to Haji no Mino and was the founder of the Haji clan after his creation of ''[[haniwa]].''<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nomi no Sukune • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/|access-date=2021-09-01|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>
== Legacy == A mural of him, removed from the old [[National Stadium (Tokyo)|National Stadium]] in 2014, was put on display at the new stadium in 2019.<ref>{{cite news|title=東京五輪向け壁画取り外し…建て替え国立競技場|url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/olympic/2020/20141105-OYT1T50114.html?from=ycont_top_txt|accessdate=8 November 2014|work=Yomiuri Shinbun|date=5 November 2014}}</ref>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Legendary sumo wrestler}} [[File:野見宿禰.jpg|thumb|200px|Nomi no Sukune by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]]]]
{{nihongo|'''Nomi no Sukune'''|野見 宿禰}} was a legendary figure in Japanese history, regarded as the founder of [[sumo]] wrestling.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book |url=https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/33014222 |title=Asashi Nihon rekishi jinbutsu jiten |date=1994 |publisher=Asahi Shinbunsha |isbn=4-02-340052-1 |at=藤原光能 |oclc=33014222}}</ref> He was the founder of the '''[[Haji clan|Haji]]''' no [[Muraji]] clan.<ref name=":3">{{Cite book |title=Nihon dai hyakka zensho |publisher=Shōgakkan |year=1989 |isbn=4-09-526001-7 |at=野見宿禰 |oclc=14970117}}</ref>
== Life == [[File:Nomi no Sukune Wrestling with Taima no Kehaya LACMA M.84.31.87.jpg|thumb|Nomi no Sukune wrestling with Taima no Kehaya (by [[Yoshitoshi]])]] Sukune is said to have lived during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] (29 BC – 70).<ref name="katano">{{cite web |title=The history of Kitano Jinja |url=http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |url-status=dead |publisher=Kitano Jinja |accessdate=2008-02-19 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080219104915/http://www12.ocn.ne.jp/~katano/e-yuisyo.html |archivedate=February 19, 2008}}</ref> Allegedly, in 23 B.C., the Emperor instructed Nomi no Sukune to deal with {{nihongo|Taima no Kehaya|当麻蹴速}} after he boasted that he was the strongest man "''under the heavens''". Nomi no Sukune engaged Taima no Kehaya in hand-to-hand combat and broke his ribs with one kick and his back with another, killing Taima no Kehaya.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Guttmann|first1=Allen|last2=Thompson|first2=Lee|title=Japanese Sports: A History|url=https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt|url-access=limited|date=2001|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|location=Honolulu, HI|isbn=9780824824143|page=[https://archive.org/details/japanesesportshi00gutt/page/n24 14]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.essortment.com/wrestlingsumoh_rvqp.htm|title=Sumo wrestling history, the Ancient Japanese sport|publisher=Essortment|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> It was not modern sumo, but he is regarded as the creator of sumo.<ref>{{cite web |title=History of Sumo Wrestling |url=http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |accessdate=2008-02-19 |publisher=Houston Chinatown |archive-date=2008-03-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080320035028/http://www.chinatownconnection.com/sumo-wrestling-history.htm |url-status=dead }}</ref><!-- He is also regarded as the human creator of [[jujutsu]].<ref name="aikido">{{cite web|url=http://www.aikido-shobukan.org/books/View.aspx?id=330|title=Canon of Judo "Principle and Technique"|author=Mifune, Kiyuzo|work=Aikido Shobukan Dojo|date=1959|accessdate=2008-02-19}}</ref> -->
He was an ancestor of [[Sugawara no Michizane]],<ref name="katano" /> great-grandfather to Haji no Mino and was the founder of the Haji clan after his creation of ''[[haniwa]].''<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nomi no Sukune • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/|access-date=2021-09-01|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>
== Legacy == A mural of him, removed from the old [[National Stadium (Tokyo)|National Stadium]] in 2014, was put on display at the new stadium in 2019.<ref>{{cite news|title=東京五輪向け壁画取り外し…建て替え国立競技場|url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/olympic/2020/20141105-OYT1T50114.html?from=ycont_top_txt|accessdate=8 November 2014|work=Yomiuri Shinbun|date=5 November 2014}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External links == * [https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nomi-no-sukune/ Nomi no Sukune] - ''History of Japan Website.''
